meta.title: LifeCareLog — Care for your life, one service at a time
meta.description: A one-person dev studio building small, meaningful services for everyday life.

hero.tagline: Designed for ease, small changes that matter
hero.headline:
Every service starts
from a real frustration.
hero.subheadline:
A frontend developer stepping out of comfort,
now building what I actually want to use — from scratch.
hero.brandPill: Care for your life, in every log

philosophy.headline:
I build services
for anyone
who needs them.
philosophy.description:
Investment platforms, mobility apps,
blockchain accounting, edtech.
Five years of building across different domains taught me something.

What seems trivial to one person
can change someone else's entire day.

So if even a few people need it, I'll build it.
Small tools that care for the parts of life we tend to overlook.
That's why I started LifeCareLog.
philosophy.quote:
My goal is to contribute by building services
that genuinely improve people's lives.

story.chapter1Title: Five years as a frontend developer
story.chapter1:
Fractional investment platforms, vehicle search apps,
blockchain accounting, edtech content editors.

Four startups over five years.
More than ten products — all on the frontend side.

I always loved building products,
but my role was always limited to the frontend.
Building something entirely on my own felt out of reach.
story.chapter2:
One day, I was let go without much warning.

It was jarring at first — but looking back,
it gave me space to sit with a question I'd been avoiding.

"I've only ever done frontend.
Could I actually build something from start to finish on my own?"
story.chapter3:
As AI tools got better,
I thought maybe I could give it a shot.

Planning, design, backend, deployment —
everything was new. I wrestled with countless errors,
learning as I went.

It's slow and clumsy,
but I'm building services that at least one person can use,
with my own hands.
story.chapter4:
I needed a loan calculator — and got ads instead.
I tried reading a contract — and hit a wall of legal jargon.
On a tough day, there was nowhere to quietly write things down.

Starting from frustrations I've actually lived,
I'm building the things life needs — one at a time.

I can build yours too.

services.headline: What I'm building
services.subheadline:
From Plan-A to Plan-Z — building useful services for everyday life, one at a time.
Each service is a Plan, bringing one more piece of life into care.
services.vision: Three services now, but someday A to Z — I want to build tools that care for every part of life.

planC.title: Plan-C
planC.subtitle: Financial Calculators & Planner
planC.description:
Loan interest, severance pay, take-home salary...
When you search for these, ads come first.
Plan-C puts the most-needed calculators in one clean place — plus scenario planning and financial planner tools.
planC.features: Loan interest · Severance pay · Take-home salary · Savings calculator · Scenario planner

planL.title: Plan-L
planL.subtitle: AI Document Analysis
planL.description:
What does this clause actually mean? What rules apply here?
Sometimes you just want to understand before asking an expert.
AI finds relevant info and explains it in plain language.
planL.teaser: AI searches official databases and breaks down the results for you.
planL.features: Document search · Clause analysis · Plain-language summary · AI insights

planT.title: Plan-T
planT.subtitle: AI Mental Wellness App
planT.description:
How are you feeling today? Why?
When it's hard to put into words, AI listens quietly.
Plan-T is an iOS/Android app for daily mental wellness — in active development.
planT.features: Mood tracking · AI counseling · Wellness reports · Daily check-in

contact.headline: What's on your mind?
contact.subheadline:
Coffee chats, collaboration ideas, project requests — all welcome.
I read every message and reply personally.
